What is the best way to center a div in CSS?

The most reliable modern approach is flexbox: .parent { display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; }

This works in all modern browsers and is the recommended approach for 2024.

What about grid? I've heard that is better for two-dimensional layouts.

Grid is excellent for two-dimensional layouts: .parent { display: grid; place-items: center; }

Both are fully supported. Choose based on your overall layout needs.

Absolute positioning fallback for old browsers: .child { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); }

But for production in 2024, flexbox or grid is strongly preferred.
